methods.method_name: Quaternary Malacofauna Collection and Initial Processing
methods.description: Process for collecting Quaternary malacofauna from sediments, including individual specimen collection, bulk sampling, and initial mechanical extraction and determination based on preservation.

steps.description: Quaternary malacofauna was collected from sediments in two ways: as individual specimens (visible to the naked eye) and in bulk samples of 3 kg.The specimens visible to the naked eye were collected individually.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect individual malacofauna specimens visible to the naked eye from sediment.

steps.description: Quaternary malacofauna was collected from sediments in two ways: as individual specimens (visible to the naked eye) and in bulk samples of 3 kg.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect bulk sediment samples (3 kg) for malacofauna extraction.

steps.description: During the mechanical processing and extraction of individual fossils from the loess sediments, hand tools, such as trowels, knives and brushes, were used.
steps.searchable_summary: Mechanically extract individual fossils from loess sediment using hand tools.

steps.description: The taxonomical determination of the "naturally prepared fossils" was done depending on the degree of preservation, on the genus or species level.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform initial taxonomic determination of fossils based on preservation degree (genus or species level).
outputs: Fossils with initial taxonomic level determination

methods.method_name: Laboratory Preparation and Identification of Fossil Malacofauna
methods.description: Laboratory procedures for preparing, restoring, cleaning, and identifying extracted fossil malacofauna specimens using microscopy, comparative collections, and literature.

steps.description: The extracted fossil snails with preserved sculpture were studied and prepared again in the laboratory.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform laboratory preparation and study of extracted fossil snails with preserved sculpture.
inputs: Extracted fossil snails with preserved sculpture

steps.description: The restoration of the broken shell fragments was made by glue and polish.
steps.searchable_summary: Restore broken fossil shell fragments using glue and polish.

steps.description: Employing table lenses, the extremely fragile and tiny specimens were "quickly" transferred in order to preserve the shell specimens.
steps.searchable_summary: Carefully transfer extremely fragile and tiny fossil specimens using table lenses for preservation.
inputs: Extremely fragile and tiny fossil specimens

steps.description: In order to perform the identification of macrofauna with stronger shells, when it was necessary to determine the elements of oral aperture, regularity and structure, these specimens were washed with 3% H 2 O 2 .
steps.searchable_summary: Wash stronger macrofauna fossil shells with 3% H2O2 for identification purposes.

steps.description: The fossil material was identified under binocular lenses, using the comparative collection of fossil and recent molluscs from the Collection of Natural History Museum, Belgrade, collected by PETAR PAVLO}, as well as foreign literature (BROHMER et al. 1962;GROSSU 1956GROSSU , 1993;;FRANK 2004;LO@EK 1964;KERNEY et al. 1983KERNEY et al. , 1999;;[ILEJKO 1984;KROLOPP & SÜMEGI 1993, 2000;SOÓS 1943SOÓS , 1959;;and PFLEGER 2000).
steps.searchable_summary: Identify fossil malacofauna specimens using binocular lenses, comparative collections, and taxonomic literature.

methods.description: Procedures for analyzing the identified malacofauna data, including using ecological valences, calculating percentages, and performing statistical processing.

steps.description: During the preparation of the histogram, ecological valences for species were used according to LO@EK (1964).
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain and use ecological valence data for identified species according to LO@EK (1964).
outputs: Ecological valence data associated with species

steps.description: In the text, the percent representation is shown in parentheses after the name of the species.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate the percentage representation of each identified species.
inputs: List of identified species and their counts/frequencies

steps.description: The statistic processing of the results is shown Table 1 and in the histogram in Fig. 7.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform statistical analysis on the processed malacofauna data.
outputs: Statistical results (e.g., summary statistics, diversity indices)

methods.description: Procedure for cataloging and storing the collected and identified fossil material.

steps.description: The material was inventoried and labeled under the Inventory number K 6306-6402.
steps.searchable_summary: Inventory and label collected and processed material with unique inventory numbers.

steps.description: and kept in the collection of the Natural History Museum, Belgrade.
steps.searchable_summary: Store inventoried material in the designated collection.
